title: Multiband superconductivity in the kagome-lattice superconductor Re2Zr
abstract: We present a first-principles investigation of the electronic structure, lattice dynamics, electron-phonon coupling, and superconducting properties of the hexagonal kagome-lattice compound Re$_2$Zr, in which time-reversal symmetry breaking and unconventional pairing have recently been proposed. We examine whether superconductivity in Re$_2$Zr can be explained within a conventional phonon-mediated framework by performing fully ab initio calculations within the density functional theory for superconductors. The electronic structure is characterized by a mixture of seven two- and three-dimensional Fermi surface sheets, giving rise to multiband superconductivity with many overlapping superconducting gaps. A spin-orbit-induced van Hove singularity is identified in the density of states near $E_F$. The electron-phonon interaction is moderately strong, with a coupling constant $λ\simeq 0.88$. Superconducting gap calculations reveal significant anisotropy and a broad distribution of gap values across different Fermi surface sheets. Spin fluctuations introduce additional depairing effects and reduce the critical temperature from 7.7 K to 6.4 K, in excellent agreement with the experimental value of 6.65 K. Taken together, our results show that the superconducting energy scale in Re$_2$Zr can be quantitatively reproduced within a conventional phonon-mediated framework, while the resulting state exhibits pronounced multiband and anisotropic gap structure. The origin of the experimentally suggested time-reversal symmetry breaking, however, remains an open question.
summary: This work investigates superconductivity in Re2Zr using advanced first-principles calculations to test conventional phonon-mediated theories. The results show that the superconducting energy scale is quantitatively reproduced by this model, revealing strong multiband and anisotropic gap features. Crucially, including spin fluctuations successfully lowers the predicted critical temperature to match experimental values, though it cannot explain the suggested time-reversal symmetry breaking.
main_problem: To determine if superconductivity in Re2Zr, despite experimental hints of unconventional pairing (like time-reversal symmetry breaking), can be quantitatively explained using a conventional phonon-mediated framework.
main_result: The superconducting energy scale is successfully reproduced within a conventional phonon-mediated model, revealing pronounced multiband and anisotropic gap structures. However, the origin of the suggested time-reversal symmetry breaking remains an open question.
method: First-principles calculations using Density Functional Theory for superconductors (SCDFT) were employed to calculate electronic structure, electron-phonon coupling ($\lambda$), and superconducting gaps ($\Delta$).
model_or_system: The study focuses on the hexagonal kagome-lattice superconductor Re2Zr. Calculations incorporate spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and treat the system as a multiband superconductor with seven distinct Fermi surface sheets.
key_observables: Superconducting critical temperature ($T_c$), electron-phonon coupling constant ($\lambda \simeq 0.88$), superconducting gap anisotropy, and the density of states at the Fermi level ($N(E_F)$).
important_parameters: Experimental $T_c = 6.65 	ext{ K}$; calculated $\lambda$; spin fluctuation effects (which reduce $T_c$ from $7.7 	ext{ K}$ to $6.4 	ext{ K}$).
main_assumptions: The primary assumption is the validity of a conventional phonon-mediated pairing mechanism within the singlet state approximation, despite discrepancies with some experimental observations.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate electronic band structures (with/without SOC), Fermi surface sheets showing mixed 2D-3D character, and gap distributions confirming multiband anisotropy across different FS sheets.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the electronic structure using DFT with SOC, then calculating electron-phonon coupling ($\lambda$), and finally solving the superconducting gap equation (SCDFT) while incorporating spin fluctuation corrections to match the experimental $T_c$.
why_interesting: While focused on conventional BCS theory, the explicit calculation of multiband effects, anisotropic gaps, and the quantitative inclusion of electron-phonon coupling alongside spin fluctuations provides a rich theoretical framework for understanding complex pairing mechanisms in strongly correlated materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Re2Zr unit cell with gray Re-1, red Re-2 and green Zr atoms, (b) electron density map in a plane with Re-2 atoms forming the kagome lattice, (c) supercell 2x2x1 highlighting the kagome lattice. Visualized using XCrysDen [6].
FIG. 2. Re2Zr electronic bands and total density of states calculated with and without spin-orbit coupling.
FIG. 3. Re2Zr Fermi surface colored with Fermi velocity. Visualized using Fermisurfer [45].
FIG. 4. Re2Zr electronic density of states projected onto atomic orbitals.

FIG. 6. (left axis) Re2Zr Eliashberg function with normalized phonon density of states ( R ωmax 0 F(ω)dω = R ωmax 0 α2F(ω)dω). (right axis) Cumulative electron-phonon coupling constant λ(ω) = 2 R ω 0 α2F (ω′)
